libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No environment variables or system properties are required. Tests should target APIs present in Spring Framework 6; do not try to preserve Spring 5-era remoting/EJB/WebLogic/WebSphere tests whose referenced classes were removed from spring-context 6.0.2.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8772 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.springframework:spring-context:6.0.2; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: spring-context contains optional integrations for Jakarta annotations/EJB, JPA, Groovy, Kotlin, and application-server classloaders; tests that intentionally exercise those optional paths may need matching test APIs or local stubs, but they are not required for baseline meaningful coverage.
The reported javac failures are consistent with removed Spring 5 classes rather than missing setup, so adding dependencies is unlikely to fix those tests.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Resolved spring-context 6.0.2 has only Spring compile dependencies and meaningful core ApplicationContext coverage is reachable without extra Maven dependencies, Docker images, or environment/system properties.
